text:
A shy girl named Millie lived by a forest. She loved animals—Illinois owls, in particular. One day she saw an owl with a bright collar. Wanting to get close without disturbing the owl's solitude, she began observing from afar. She learned that the owl's favorite spot was the old oak tree at dusk. Whenever she saw it, she'd hold her breath.

One night the owl had a nest of little owl kits. They seemed frightened of humans. Millie got a stuffed owl owl of her own and kept it just there on the porch. One moonlit night, she was watching when the largest owl fathered a tiny owl who stared straight into her eyes. Over time, the owl grew bold and brave under Millie's gentle watch. Even when she fed them nightworms, they didn't fly away. In return, they'd flap their wings gently, chasing each other around.

One springtime dawn, just as the owl family awoke, she
